name: cfgrib (GRIB2 to CF Conventions via xarray)

performance.notes: eccodes C library backend; I/O-bound; large GRIB2 files common (GBs)

agent_readiness.notes: Read GRIB2 weather/climate files into xarray via CF conventions. import cfgrib. ds = xr.open_dataset('era5.grib', engine='cfgrib'). ds = cfgrib.open_dataset('era5.grib'). Multiple datasets: cfgrib.open_datasets('era5.grib') for files with mixed types. filter_by_keys={'typeOfLevel': 'surface', 'shortName': '2t'}. Requires eccodes C library (conda: eccodes). ERA5, ECMWF, GFS, NWP data. indexpath for caching GRIB index. Not all GRIB2 messages decode cleanly.

gotchas: Requires eccodes C library (not pip-installable — use conda or apt)
Mixed-type GRIB files require open_datasets() not open_dataset()
filter_by_keys needed to select specific variables
Index files (.idx) left in same directory as GRIB files
Some GRIB2 messages fail to decode — error messages are cryptic
best_when: Reading ERA5, GFS, ECMWF GRIB2 weather/climate data into xarray
When GRIB2 is the only available format for meteorological data
Filtering specific variables from large multi-variable GRIB files
avoid_when: When eccodes installation is not possible
Production pipelines (fragile on complex GRIB files)
summary: cfgrib reads GRIB2 weather/climate files into xarray via CF conventions. xr.open_dataset('era5.grib', engine='cfgrib'). filter_by_keys for variable selection. cfgrib.open_datasets() for mixed-type files. Apache-2.0 by ECMWF. Requires eccodes C library (conda install). Standard for ERA5/ECMWF/GFS data in xarray workflows. Mixed-type GRIB files need open_datasets(); index files written to same directory.
